reviews.review_text: *Batman: Arkham City* is an outstanding superhero game that improves on almost every aspect of its predecessor. The open-world design of Arkham City is immersive, allowing players to explore a detailed and atmospheric version of Gotham. Combat feels smooth, fast, and rewarding, while the stealth mechanics offer plenty of strategic options. The story is engaging from start to finish, featuring memorable villains and excellent voice acting, especially from Batman and the Joker. Side missions add depth and variety, making the world feel alive. Overall, Arkham City delivers an unforgettable experience and remains one of the best Batman games ever made.

Around 5 hours into Batman: Arkham City and I completely understand why people still call this one of the best superhero games ever made.

The atmosphere is honestly incredible. Gliding around Arkham City at night genuinely makes you FEEL like Batman, and the game still looks surprisingly good even today. The combat is insanely addictive too — once the combos start flowing, it’s hard to stop playing.

The story has been amazing so far, and every time Batman shows up in a cutscene he somehow manages to look cool as hell. There were multiple moments already where I just sat there thinking “yeah this is peak Batman.”

Some of the stealth/predator sections can get stressful sometimes, but in a good way. It really makes you think before jumping into a room full of enemies.

I played Arkham Asylum before this, and Arkham City honestly feels like a huge upgrade in scale and freedom. It still holds up better than a lot of modern superhero games.

Definitely recommend it, especially if you can grab it on discount. If you’re a Batman fan, this game is almost mandatory.

Really good game. This game makes you really FEEL like Batman and the story is impeccable. I really liked the mix between Batmans POV and the switches with Catwoman. Arkham Asylum is a great game but Arkham City is a big upgrade in terms of openworld and story aswell as combat design and exploration capabilities with the fast grapple thingymajig. 

A strong 9/10!
Great game, everything Arkham Asylum did, City does better. Highly recommended
Despite many people liking it and saying it's the best of the franchise, I'd say it's good but not *that* good
The story is strong, with great villains and standout voice acting from Batman and the Joker. Side missions add variety, making Gotham feel alive. Still one of the best Batman games ever made.
Great atmosphere, great game.

Replaying Arkham City for the first time since I played and beat it back in 2015 on the Nintendo Wii U was an absolute blast. It was exactly how I remembered it, and the story was still captivating. It holds up well even today. The city, however, was a bit choppy, but it was still enjoyable to watch. Everything else was amazing. The combat was just as good as I remembered, with the ability to jump around, punch enemies, and even fly around. This game was simply fun, and I’m glad I had the chance to replay Arkham City again.

Takes everything great about the original Arkham Asylum and improves on it, especially the bosses, which are actually interesting and well designed this time around! The gameplay remains intuitive and well-designed in movement, combat and puzzles. Arkham City as an open world is perfect. It's vast and fun to explore, but it's still small enough to allow the smaller details to shine and not feel like a bloated Ubisoft mess. Story wise, the game carries over some more problematic aspects of "edgy Batman," but even those elements are at least used with somewhat thematic depth. The entire Ra's Al Ghul plotline is still orientalist, but here more in the purpose of being anti-imperialist. The camera constantly oggling the women characters that were already designed using one hand is still ridiculous, but at the very least the story makes it clear that Batman would be dead without the women in his life by now and they all get enough screentime to not be one-dimensional. The most interesting, disturbing and relevant plotline is still the Nazi-coded Hugo Strange and his TYGER squad, an entire legally authorized domestic terrorist organization allowed to kill with impunity and arrest whomever they please (what does that remind you of, I wonder?) It's not the best Batman story, imo, but it does have some great stuff in it. The voice cast kill it, as usual. Kevin Conroy (RIP) shall always be my favourite Batman and I very much enjoyed the performances of Mark Hamill, Grey Griffin, Kimberly Brooks, Corey Burton, Nolan North, Maurice LaMarche and everyone else. Tara Strong still doesn't hold a candle to Arleen Sorkin as Harley Quinn, though, and the difference in Sorkin's departure after Asylum is sorely felt.
The DLCs are fun, but nothing that exciting. They're all very short in the grand scheme of things, though I do like how Catwoman's DLC is intervowen in the main story.

I first began the Arkham series with Knight, enjoyed it enough to then play Asylum then (skipped out on Origins.) But for whatever reason, I felt like this game was sort of a let down. I loved the almost rusty, Alcatraz inspiration for Asylum (due to the fact that it quite literally IS just Gotham's Alcatraz,) and I also found the linearity of Asylum strange yet enjoyable compared to Knight. Knight on the other hand gives you a nice amount of freedom, and absolutely incredible graphics for 2015, and the story feels mostly okay for how the game wants it to go (you could argue it's maybe a bit odd how they'll introduce Most Wanted missions at certain points in the main story.) Yet Arkham City just didn't really land right with me. For starters, you don't get as interesting a cutscene as Batman rushing to Arkham Asylum, or Batman literally burning the Joker after he dies of a Titan overdose (Important thing in Asylum and City,) but City's starting cutscene (if I remember correctly) is Batman arguing against the opening of Arkham City for whatever reason (Arkham City is basically just Detroit, you can neither leave or quite get in, and compared to Arkham Asylum you just get heavily screwed over by the leading enforcer of Arkham Asylum, Hugo Strange,) off the bat I thought not only was it a fairly good idea, but also it made no sense Batman was arguing against Arkham Asylum, he's not okay with Gotham's greatest villains fighting gang wars, but killing one person and preventing the deaths of hundreds is too much? I thought the rest of the story was kind of just... boring, especially the ending where I'm pretty sure you: Don't fight Hugo Strange; Don't fight Ra's al ghul, (he plays a pretty important role for a whopping 45 minutes or so,); Don't even fight the JOKER or TWO-FACE or the PENGUIN, any of the important crime lords in the city, you wanna know who you fight? Take a guess. You have your guess? You might be wrong, you fight Clay Face. Of ALL of the cool characters introduced in Arkham Asylum and Arkham City, you get to fight Clay Face instead of your final face off with the Joker. It may sound like I hated this game, I didn't, but it certainly is the weakest of the main 3 Arkham games. I did like some of the Cat woman sections, the Penguin segment for Batman was alright, and I thought parts of Mr. Freeze's story were good, but it's just not replayable. I know this is some peoples favorite Arkham game, and I just think its not for me. 7.5/10.
even after 15 years this game is still the at the top spot in superhero genre just go for it i dont need to write anything to define its greatness

[b] Batman: Arkham city GOTY:[/b] Really good game, i played Arkham ASYLUM and CITY Consecutively amd i must say and most player agree the game mechanics and environment is wayy too improved than the ASYLUM game feel smooth, Story is more detailed Although both game walkthrough on video platforms is around 6hours but it felt story completed a little faster in this... but they added more riddles and side quest and even a story continuity named harley quinn's revenge which also great
overall AWESOME game

Batman Arkham City is probably one of the best superhero games in the world. Everything that made Arkham Asylum great was made greater in Arkham City, and everything that made Arkham Asylum bad was improved upon. The music is FANTASTIC. The gameplay is AMAZING. The traversal is STELLAR And the combat. The combat is GLORIOUS> More characters. More map. More story. To quote Homelander. "I gotta tell you. It was perfect. Perfect. Everything down to the last minute details." If I was to rate this game out of 10. I'd give it a 50. Well worth the price of purchase, and well worth the hours spent playing. 

Put it like this. I have played this game on the PS3, Xbox360 and PC. And not just once each. A multitude of times.
goated game......i mean what else is there to say???? its batman made by an amazing studio that showed true passion

This still holds up way better than most games from its time. The combat is smooth, fast, and just feels good every time you get into a fight. It’s simple enough to jump into, but there’s still enough timing, gadgets, counters, and enemy variety to make you feel like you’re actually playing smart, not just mashing buttons.

The city is also a huge part of why it works. Gliding over rooftops, dropping into fights, sneaking around, solving Riddler stuff, and running into different villains makes the whole place feel alive in that dark Batman way. The atmosphere is great, the voice acting is great, and the story keeps moving without feeling like filler.

It’s one of those games where the main missions are fun, but you also keep getting distracted by side content and don’t really mind. Very easy recommendation, especially if you like Batman or just want a strong action game that still feels good today.
probably the best Arkham game.....  it's the one I replay the most, they're all actually good.

Batman: Arkham Asylum was good. Batman: Arkham City is absolutely fantastic. Dear reader, I basically NEVER do New Game + in any game. Ever. As things stand, I'm one of the 4.3% of people who have completed it in Batman: Arkham City. The gameplay is incredible, improving on the already wonderful formula presented to us in Arkham Asylum. The presentation is perfect, the world-building is perfect. Everything about this game is worth experiencing, and you can do it RIGHT NOW for only $20. In a world where a movie ticket costs more than half of that, this game is an absolute steal at its price point.

It should be noted, for those of us old enough to remember Batman: The Animated Series and all of the excellent things that came from it, that this is one of the last places you can go to hear Kevin Conroy's iconic portrayal of the Batman alongside Mark Hamill's Joker. Hearing these two masters of their craft portray these characters in the same game is worth the price of admission by itself, to say nothing of the rest of the cast. Maurice LaMarche as Mr. Freeze? Fred Tatasciore as Bane? Stana Katic as Talia Al Ghul? Nolan North as the Penguin? 

Why are you still reading this?!

Buy this game. You won't be disappointed, except when it's over and you realize that this one was probably the best in the entire series. All of the Arkham games are worth playing, but none of them quite measure up to this one. There is an easy 60 hours of AAA entertainment to be had here if you decide to take your time and do all of the sidequests and minigames, and absolutely all of it is a joy to play through. 

Rest in peace, Kevin Conroy, and thanks for being the voice behind the Bat.
Arkham City at 60 fps is an absolute delight. This game looks and plays great on modern hardware. If you're a Batman fan, this game is a must.
This game feels like a movie. Great story with a great atmosphere and music to back it up. This is as epic as it gets

2026 im someone who doesnt like old games because of graphics and this game exploted my expectations 
experience 8/10
world is pretty amazing combat is amazing also i am falling in love with this game

Playing this for the first time in 2026 after hearing only good things about it. I can safely say it holds up after all this time later. It is an absolutely amazing open world game and is definitely in my Top 10 games of all time.

Batman: Arkham City GOTY is one of the best superhero games ever made.

The combat still feels incredibly satisfying, the stealth sections make you actually feel like Batman, and the open-world Gotham atmosphere is perfect. Flying across Arkham City at night while hunting criminals never gets old.

The story is amazing too, with some of the best versions of Joker, Catwoman, and other iconic villains. The GOTY edition adds even more content, making an already fantastic game even better.

Even years later, this game holds up surprisingly well.

If you like Batman even a little, this is a must-play.

9.5/10

It's the best game of the series. Greatly improved gameplay from Arkham Asylum, excellent story and pacing, an insane amount of extra unlockable content (such as extra challenges, concept art, character statues, etc.) as you obtain collectibles, and you get a ton of hours out of it. WELL worth the 20 bucks, but it goes on sale pretty frequently for really cheap. It looks fantastic and runs exceptionally well even on low-end hardware. This is better than probably every current AAA title right now

didn't know cosmetic mods affects achievements, oh well. anyway, major improvement from the previous title, combat and movement controls felt way better and way smoother than asylum (obv), also nice to have other bat fam member as a playable character.

My most played Arkham Game since I was obsessed with this when it launched back in the day. Played it 3 times straight which is testament to how replayable it is. And now on my 6th playthrough its not quite my favorite arkham game since Knight has the best gameplay, graphics and freedom. But man is it worth playing. interior levels are too plentiful and back tracking could be a bore is my only two issues with it.

Fantastic game to jump into right after playing Arkham Asylum! The open world layout compliments the dark storytelling of Batman.

His gadgets really makes exploring the map a smooth, exciting experience. Gliding through Arkham was EXACTLY what I was looking for! The combat was smoother in this game than Arkham Asylum as well and I love the introduction of more characters. The voice acting was perfect in my opinion and the ending of the game was worth the playthrough. 

100% recommend rate from me!
The games story is one of the best ive played the side missions are good only the riddler ones get a bit annoying sometimes
Favorite Batman Game! Very nostalgic. The opening song is amazing as well

8/10

Batman arkham city is the 2nd game of the Arkham series, incredibly worth playing in 2026. it is a step-up evolution from asylum and Open world. Graphically and much smoother compared its predecessor. the Story is unique, twisting, the upgrades are awesome, and the combat is incredibly seamless.

My issues with it would be challenges being a hinderance to overall Completion progress, and the twists in story , but aside that every part of it is awesome

After 8 playthroughs and 300 hours across xbox 360, xbox one and ps4 i can't recommend this game anymore, the boss fights are serviceable not bad like arkham asylums or arkham knights, deadshots "boss fight" was done so dirty i guess rocksteady ran out of time and had to rush this boss fight.

The story is good but hugo strange has 2 scenes  and that's it very disappointing, ra's al ghul being the mastermind and not strange was a very weird writing choice, they brought hugo strange into a batman game a character i never thought would make it into movie let alone a batman game, he could have been a true nightmare to batman he should have gotten more scenes, also strange knows batman is bruce wayne but he never used that as blackmail to stop him from interfering in his plans nor did he try to release his identity to gotham???????

The riddler trophies in this game and arkham origins suck so much, just non stop confusing puzzles.

The side quests are lackluster at best and terrible at worst, zsasz side quest is so awful i dread hearing that stupid phone, mad hatters quest is great, hush is great too prob my fav.

The world of arkham city is boring to explore there is a lack of cool or interesting locations i think? or it is just boring to explore in general.

All in all a great first attempt at a open world game prob should have stuck to something akin to arkham asylums level design.

Still i will give this a thumbs up it is not the worst game in the series that prob goes to arkham knight.
overall  a peak game in general and it is a nicely crafted world of gotham

i love this game, i do feel like the map is not as good as arkham origins but i do love it, i feel like the game is  can be hard when it comes to combat which i think its quite cool, as it feels like batmans health is more limited compared to all the games in the franchise, i didnt really like the catwoman section after the ending of the game as she was extremely weak, it took about 3 shots from a enemy to kill her, if she shared the same durability as your upgraded batman i think that section couldve been better
10/10 ,one of the best sequels ever made. Up there with portal 2 and borderlands 2.

The quintessential game about superheroes, it is just classic, a game that every fan of Batman or superheroes has to try and finish at least once. The predecessor of the Spider-Man videogames and a franchise that revolutionized what a superhero videogame should be. I can't recommend it enough

Amazing game, has so many batman villains and manages to juggle them all, while keeping a consistent pacing, which is impressive because they have, in the main story only, two face, joker, harley quin, ra's al ghul, talia al ghul, penguin, dr. hugo strange, mr freeze, and clayface, all in this single game, which is impressive, and the combat is peak

finally manage to experience arkhamverse its really good from gameplay to story wise. On my way to play Arkham Knight

11/10 story, felt like new even after replaying it
The Batman:Arkham series made me for of a batman fan than movies could ever.

Play the previous games in order to understand the game more deeply.

I really liked this game
The story,gameplay and narrative were all great.
Sometimes the missions feel like a carrot on a stick but it not much of a biggie

- Story is really good
 - Mechanics are fun (Fighting in combat and Gliding/Grappling around the city)
 - Incredible game for its age!

One of the best sequel games ever played 

Great story, solid stealth sections, and improved combat from Asylum

I would recommend this game to literally anyone.

I thought I would not enjoy this game (as much) having played Arkham Knight first quite a while back. I can safely say that was incorrect. Very good game, albeit I did not 100% it, I did play many of the side missions and completed the main story. 

Blade evasion combat controls on keyboard are very awkward. If that's my only complaint, you know it's great otherwise. Probably a skill issue!
DUDEEEEEEE how this game can be that good i'm not even a big batman but this game make me love him (Best game of the Arkham trilogy)
Definitive comic book batman experience. I just wish they didn't repeat Joker in all the games

This is probably the most accurate adaptation of the Batman mythos that we're ever going to get in game form. The story's a lot better than Asylum and still very gamey (that's a plus because that means that the story actually offers you clear objectives to follow throughout). The side quests provide a lot more to do and are a lot better than they are in Arkham Origins in complementing the main storyline. The Deadshot side quest was annoying to get though as the second victim literally took WAY too long to occur (I don't like when progression is gated like this and I don't think anyone does. Though the map is easier to navigate than in Arkham Origins, Arkham Asylum is still BY FAR the best level design in the entire Arkham series so far, at least in terms of being able to find your way around (barring that stupid elevator shaft) being that it was designed in the style of a 3D metriodvania. The only game I've ever completed the Riddler stuff for is Arkham Asylum simply because that kind of collectible side quest is always something I find tedious in Open World games (and I would say that the rest of the Arkham games are Open World or at least Open Zone games). Though character progression and mechanics are a lot better in this game, I'm not sure whether I would take it over the more linear experience of Asylum. Harley's revenge actually gave her something to do, though that speaks to just how weakly Harley is written in the main game. Still one of the best superhero games of all time, but I was surprised by how many issues I found on just a game design level. Relatively well optimised, so I don't think that's a problem.

Overall, should you play it? Of course, it's awesome... I just think that this game has probably aged the worst out of the mainline Arkham games.

Before I played this game, I had played games similar to it, for example, on Xbox 360, Dead pool Spider-Man while on PS4 Spider-Man. That is why I knew I would like it. Honestly, I couldn't play the Xbox 360 version properly, so I played it again from the beginning. This game is a reason for this genre. Personally, I really liked this game and I would recommend it to anyone who likes this genre. Despite this, it has its downsides, for example: its atmosphere is slightly worse than the previous game; the story, even though I liked it, was still a bit rushed. I wouldn't change anything about this game.
100% completion!!!
10/10
Great game.

It's a good story, amazing gameplay, great atmosphere. I mean who doesn't like batman games? 9/10.
Must play.

I played this when it was released on Xbox 360. I picked it back up when Steam was having an amazing deal for a couple bucks. I’ve put over 40 hours into this game and I absolutely adore this game. I play it on my Ayn Odin 2 Portal and it’s been a wonderful experience.

The shift from Asylums more linear approach to City's open world Allowed for an almost immersive sim approach to many of the situations. The sand box made great use of Batmans gadgets and made for a lot of fun fleshed out predator sections. Where Origins predator sections felt rng reliant the ones here feel purposeful. I always felt in control and like i was Batman. 

The story had a good pacing till the end. It kind of just ends where i thought there was at least a few hours left. Joker as always steals the show. 

Gameplay has a great flow to it and proper impact. Taking down groups always felt satisfying. 

TLDR: Overall a very solid sequel to an already amazing start.

An AWESOME game!! This game holds up 14 years later. For someone that has a large backlog of single player games I missed out on, I got glued to this one. I played a lot of multiplayer games once I got into pc gaming in 2009 and guess I let a lot of the single player games take a back burner. Well getting older, living the family life, and not seeing as much of my friends online anymore has brought me back around to playing all the games I have missed. Been slowly playing franchise after franchise in chronological order. Can't wait to get on Arkham Knight.
you can tell the games 15y old...but the story is AMAZING. The game really lives up to the hype

Only 25% in but I'm already hooked. The Combat feels great once it clicks(expect nothing less from Arkham Series),I feel like I've spent way too long just gliding around the city instead of doing actual missions. Story seems to have a bigger roster of villains which is cool. Map is a massive compared to previous game but its bit confusing at first ngl. Will update when I finish but so far easily worth it

I replayed this earlier this year. And it's still my favourite of the Arkham games. I much prefer this over Arkham Knight, mostly owing to the lack of Batmobile sections (really not a fan of the car/tank puzzles and combat.) 

I think the plot here is also a strength. It explores new villains, venomous rivalries and unlikely alliances, but does so with enough restraint to remain focused on the core narrative.

The side-characters in this game are another highlight. Nightwing, Robin and Catwoman all offer unique gameplay styles with engaging side-plots.

Definitely worth playing, even in 2026. An all time great in the open world superhero genre.
greatest game i’ve ever played, top notch story with awesome gameplay and the graphics and aesthetics are just so nice to look at. truly spectacular game and i highly recommend!
I actually enjoyed this game a bit more than Arkham orgins. The story is better imo. Also fun to be able to play as catwoman. Even though these games are old, they still hold up and are very fun.

I've never been a huge comics fan, and so Batman was probably the only classic superhero that I actually related to. And I can only imagine what a treat this game is for die-hard fans of his, with such attention to the world-building, the aesthetics, and gameplay. In my crooked and sluggish hands though the world's moodiest detective died a hundred times to ordinary thugs... it's rather sophisticated and does require planning and poise to make the most of all the gadgets and talents. Be advised that you cannot just brute force your way out of trouble!
Gotham also has dozens of side-quests for those seeking to extend their experience, a multitude of Riddler's puzzles, and much more lore and references that I could possibly get. Finally, what I enjoyed the most is how smooth the hero's animations and movement really are, the developers really nailed what it means to be Batman. I couldn't help but enjoy every punch and every dive onto unsuspecting enemies.

A short recommendation because this game doesn't need much. It is a bigger Arkham Asylum, better in many aspects. I'm just getting started playing but if you like Batman and action fighting games, you will like this game. If you are a fan of Batman, this game is an important part of your collection. There are a lot of extra features packed in. Here are some of the Pros:

Pros
 - Refined concept from Arkham Asylum, it feels like a polished combat experience
 - Music is top-notch, a different vibe from many other game
 - There's a vast and interesting and engaging singe player campaign
 - There are many features outside the single player campaign (extra challenges, images, etc.)
 - This is the first game that lets me play as Catwoman and she is cool in combat.
 - Enough for now! I love the game and I feel like I've only scratched the surface.
 - It makes you genuinely feel like Batman.
Real fun, just ignore ridler's revenge campaign challenges

Good game, old but still very fun.

If you love Batman you will love this game

Batman: Arkham City is one of the best superhero games ever made. The gameplay feels perfect once you learn the combo system, counters, gadgets, and how to react to enemy attacks. It’s not a grinding game at all — the combat stays fun from start to finish.

The story is excellent, and I also loved how the game gives focus to different characters like Catwoman and Robin, adding more depth to the overall experience. The stealth gameplay is what truly makes this game special. You can’t just rush enemies blindly, especially when guns can destroy your health quickly, so every fight becomes a mix of dodging, attacking, countering, and planning your moves carefully.

Even in 2026, Batman: Arkham City still holds up as one of the greatest action games ever made.
Great game.  Runs great.  Story was good.  Controls good and Kevin Conroy and Mark Hamill.
catwoman dialogue is cringe but the game is a masterpiece other than that

I've honestly played this on my Xbox 360 before which is why I don't have as many hours on Steam - but this game is frickin incredible. I'm yet to even try out the Robin and Catwoman parts of the game so I'm pretty excited for those parts too!
You should definitely play this game.

While this entry is the most beloved it is my least favourite. But that does not understate the amount of content that the game packs with the best story in the trilogy. The amount of background lore and story happening outside of the main quest is outstanding.

The gameplay is refined so much compared to Arkham Asylum and is a joy to play through start to finish.

Easy recommend.
Even after so many years, this game feels fresh and smooth like a new release. The combat system being the standout with excellent story to top it  off

While this may be my least favorite arkham game (hot take i know) it is still an iconic and important game not just for batman and superhero fans but for gamers in general. this game took what worked with the gameplay in asylum and improved on it in every single way. While i have my issues with the game (Lackluster side missions, an obnoxious amount of riddler trophies, catwomans stealth and traversal, the two face boss fight, and bane) this game is still a must play for all gamers.
stealth gaming and use of batman's gadgets is a fun experience

I genuinely like this game, as a fan of batman and the entire series its 100% worth picking up. Its got everything you want from a batman game, Action, Story and All the gadgets you can think of. 


The combat is fluid and satisfying with skills to be unlocked and many different enemies to face off against.

The story is interesting with many twists and turns and different missions to play though.

The graphics hold up even by todays standard and the world is vibrant and dynamic.

My only complains would be that there a couple of bugs from when the game was ported to pc but there are plenty of forums and help online to fix these issues.

Overall Id say its worth picking up if your'e a fan of batman and looking for a satisfying experience.

ohhhhh mahn what a game actually the fighting mechanisms are so fluid and buttery smooth i really loveed this game ,no i recommend everyone to play this game it is actually a game that everyone should who loves gaming. i really enjoyed this game. i don't know how much time i played . i have no ideas of hours i have played. loved this game sooooooo much
really good game played batman arkham asylum and it was great, this is no different. one of the best action games i have every played

So far, I'm roughly 7 hours into the game. [spoiler] (Just beat The Penguin and I'm after Ra's Al Gul) [/spoiler]

The graphics are decent. I wish we had more granular graphics settings to change LOD biases, change fog, change depth of field, enhance mesh geometry,  enhance model polycount, etc. While dated, the overall visual style holds up well. There are definitely some models and characters that were obviously cut down to improve performance. 



The gameplay was unbearable for a while due to it being locked at 60FPS. I know it doesn't sound that bad, but it was introducing a ton of input latency. I have no empirical data on how much latency, but it felt well over 100ms. This made countering attacks very difficult. Strangely enough, the original Arkham Asylum game was capped at 60FPS and didn't have this horrible input latency problem. [spoiler] I suspect this is due to the forced frame smoothing. It's probably some variant of vsync. I don't know anyone who likes vsync. Along with motion blur, vsync is the herpes of the graphics settings world.[/spoiler] It was borderline unplayable until I edited an engine.ini file within the game's directory to disable frame smoothing and to bump the FPS cap to what my monitor supports. There is also a very important texture streaming flag that must be changed. It's capped to use like 115MB of vRAM. That is why the entire game lags like hell when you load new areas or swing through large distances in the overworld. If you do not edit this engine.ini file then you'll have a tremendously worse experience with the game. 

As for the gameplay, it's good. Pretty standard when compared to Arkham Asylum. It's nice that you don't have to unlock every tool in Batman's arsenal. Pretty much what you unlocked in the previous game is available from the beginning here. 

The map design is nowhere near as intricate as the Asylum was. The asylum had a nearly infinite amount of rat tunnels, secrets, verticality, shortcuts, etc. that this game simply does not live up to. Riddler trophies are more abundant in this game. They are everywhere and range from freebies to impossible to figure out without a guide. 

After editing the engine.ini file, the combat is excellent. Without editing that file you're going to have a bad time. For some reason, it doesn't feel as fluid as Arkham Asylum. I think the parry windows were tightened in this release. 

That's all I feel like writing until I complete the game. 


After completing the game I still recommend it. It has its faults. It's not perfect. It was definitely rushed. For some reason the difficulty slider just increases enemy health and increases their damage dealt to you. This is the laziest and most common way developers implement "difficulty" in games. 

The Harley DLC might not be worth it, unless it is purchased during a steep sale. It's just one mission. There's basically no story to it. If you were a huge fan of the game then maybe it would be more worth your time to get. It's maybe an extra 30 minutes of play time.  

 NG+ is very boring. I'm also highly disappointed I didn't get any achievements or extra in-game loot for beating the game on the hardest difficulty. Kind of a let down. It was still a great game. I don't think it was as good as Arkham Asylum, though.

Probably the only perfect Batman adaptation out there, a better understanding of what makes the character work than most of the movies. It has the best open world of any game, not bloated or a slog to traverse it is tight and that feels like every part has a story or an easter egg to the larger batman mythos. Plus an addicting and fun gameplay style that makes you feel like you are the batman.

[i]Hush, little baby, don't say a word 
Momma's gonna kill for you the whole damn world.[/i] 

Idk, that combo-based fighting gets real old real fast. Also, feels like one too many baddies at the end. Aside from that, a solid game - some fun enough side quests, general vibe of one long weary night (gotta love such details as how the Batman's suit gets more and more damaged with time), good main story with Joker. 8 cats out of 10.
This game somehow does NOT feels outdated in 2026. An amazing sequel to Arkham Asylum with tons of gameplay, map, and story improvements. Works amazingly well in steam deck with ultra/high settings
Expands upon the greatness of asylum with an even better story and setting. The best superhero game of all time. 9.6/10.
Its a Batman Arkham game so of course is good. But in my opinion out of the three Arkham games I played (City, Origins and Knight) City is clearly the worst (in every aspect).

This is my second attempt at the second chapter of Rocksteady’s saga about the Dark Knight. I first played it 13 years ago, but back then I simply did not have enough patience to finish it. Now, however, I felt ready to return to Gotham and give it another proper chance.

Pros:
✅ It is honestly hard to believe this game originally came out in 2011. It does not feel inferior to modern titles in any meaningful way.
✅ The visuals are excellent, the character models are designed beautifully, and the dark, grimy ghetto of Arkham City looks absolutely stunning in terms of atmosphere.
✅ The music and voice acting are easily among the highest quality you can find in video games.
✅ In the end, I have to admit I was wrong. The game does not drag at all and never feels like it takes too long to get through.
✅ Every mechanic has been significantly expanded compared to Batman: Arkham Asylum. Everything that worked in the first game works even better here.
✅ The combat is, of course, very solid and reasonably demanding, fully exploiting the signature system created by the developers of this trilogy.
✅ I love the fact that the game lets you play not only as Batman, but also as other members of the Bat-Family. It adds a lot of variety, especially since the other playable characters actually feel different from Batman.
✅ The rogues’ gallery is about five times more impressive than in the first game. This time, we basically get almost all of Batman’s core villains.
✅ I respect the more complex environmental puzzles and the new tools and gadgets required to solve them.
✅ The cutscenes are well directed, while the dialogue is engaging and genuinely pleasant to listen to.
✅ The side missions are fairly simple, but quite varied, with some interesting ideas and mechanics.
✅ If the first game could be compared to a regular episode of Batman: The Animated Series, where Joker starts a riot in Arkham, then this one feels like a true apocalypse, a war, and a full-blown ride with no brakes. The story is quite solid...

Cons:
❌ ...but I found the resolution of Hugo Strange’s role as the main villain slightly unsatisfying.
❌ The game opens with the revelation that the antagonist knows Batman’s true identity, but this fact is basically ignored afterwards. It gives us an interesting opening scene and sequence, but not much beyond that.
❌ My biggest issue with the game is that I constantly got lost while trying to find the right door or passage. I got frustrated way more often than I should have simply because I had no idea where to go. The map is useless.
❌ As I mentioned, the Riddler puzzles themselves can be quite interesting, but their number is simply ridiculous. The map is absolutely littered with them. After a while, you just do not even want to look at them anymore. I openly admit that I did not feel like finding all of them, even though collecting a large number of them is tied to an actual story thread.
❌ The open world is beautiful, but there were many moments where Batman would get stuck on obstacles or fail to move through places where the real Batman would easily glide through without a problem.
❌ Stealth, while demanding and satisfying, often felt broken to me. Enemies seemed to detect me randomly at times, which forced me to reload saves whenever I wanted to complete a mission quietly.
❌ Similarly to the first game, the character progression system is, to put it mildly, not particularly impressive, although it does basically serve its purpose.

I honestly do not understand people who have much more affection for Arkham Asylum. Arkham City does basically everything better. It is a far more satisfying adventure and, in my opinion, a massive improvement over its predecessor.

I will not hide the fact that Rocksteady’s Batman series has a special place in my heart, and that is largely because of my first, rather accidental encounter with Arkham City years ago. A phenomenal game. Highly recommended, especially for comic book fans.

It´s an amazing game that adds a lot to the Batman lore. However, if you are a completionist, it's exhausting! There are too many specific trophies that you gotta be aware of and waaay too many collectables.

This is a totally biased review and I'll feel bad if i give this game a down vote but i really have an itch to scratch with this game. 
Lets just start with the positives of this game. The open world layout of this game is really well done as it offers a lot of interactive elements like quest lines and npc dialogues that make the world of Arkham more immersive for the player. The combined elements of mise en scene work together seamlessly to curate a specific mood that pairs like honey and butter within the arkham franchise. 

However, at the end of the day, while Batman will always be my number one hoe,  his character in this game feels a tad stiff and static. i couldn't shake the feeling that there was some unexplored depth, crucial within his character, that was not explicitly portrayed on screen. Most of his dialogues in the main quest are really basic and don't reveal much more about him besides from the fact that he wants to complete the mission. 

For an avid Batman fan as i am, the entire plot was atrocious and revealed many inconsistent plotholes that were never filled in. 
(SPOILERS) Call me uneducated about batman and tahlias relationship bc from what i know they arent even together within the events of the current story, so why is THE BATMAN wiling to sacrifice thousands of people to save her life. its just completely out of character for him as isnt weighing the odds and choosing the most justified option like his life legacy ? him choosing tahlia's life over thousands of people is simply unfathomable for a character like batman who actually uses his critical thinking skills and doesnt let his emotions affect his duty.

Plus, the main story's progression is utterly disjointed. i dont even know what the point is anymore like first we wanted to get a cure for batman but then all of a sudden a new event takes place which skews the entire main storyline off course and then we go on this alternate questline for like half the game until we get back on track and then somehow we will manage to get sidetrack bc of some villian is trynna fight us or smt the quest is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

While this game may be suited for more casual fans as many consider this a masterpiece, i find it lacking in many areas that are pivotal to the enjoyment of the game. I'm sure if i ungabunga through this game with no critical thinking skills, i too would take quite a liking to this game but as mentioned, my need for a foolproof plot overwhelms that thought.

Batman Arkham City is one of the 3diest game I've played, it requires 3 dimensional thinking, the way to the objective might be from top of the buildings, through the floors of the building, via the sewer/subway or using the traditional way on the ground. Finding the way to get in & out of places requires brain with good memory. 

The game runs flawlessly, No crashes, No freezing, No significant glitches. It is a a piece of art, Game of the Year worthy. It released almost 14 years ago but it still has its charms. Visuals, audio, story, voice acting and Action is all A grade. The 4 Arkham games - Asylum, City, Origins and Knight are recommended on Sale.

the game is fantastic, its clear where the next objective is, the story is fantastic, the music is just beautiful, its priced extremely low compared to new games that are far worse, this game is absolutely perfect and all the negative reviews i've read so far puzzle me because i swear these people are dumber than toddlers and the graphics still hold up to this day honestly a fantastic game only if rocksteady made a 4th game. there is no ssktjl in gotham city

Batman: Arkham City is a near-perfect evolution of everything Arkham Asylum started. Expanding from a confined setting into an open section of Gotham turned prison, the game delivers a much larger, more dynamic experience without losing what made the first so great. Gameplay remains tight and satisfying, the visuals are excellent, and the story is driven by a stacked lineup of iconic villains like Joker, Hugo Strange, Ra’s al Ghul, and Mr. Freeze. Add in a huge roster of side characters and missions, and it truly feels like a celebration of Batman’s entire universe. There’s an incredible amount of content here, and it all comes together into one of the most complete and enjoyable superhero games ever made. An essential experience for Batman fans - and just a fantastic game overall.

[h1] THE ULTIMATE DARK KNIGHT FANTASY [/h1]

[b][i]  Taking everything that made Asylum incredible and expanding it into a gorgeous, grim open-world Gotham. Arkham City delivers one of the greatest comic book storylines ever told, featuring an unforgettable rivalry with the Joker and a stellar cast of villains. Gliding through the rooftops and mastering the perfected combat is pure gaming perfection.

I am vengeance. I am the night. 10/10  [/i][/b]
